query:
now back to the design approach for issue 458: thoughts? DIAGNOSIS + DESIGN REVIEW COMPLETE — Awaiting approval.
                                                                                
  ---
  Root Cause: Orphaned-edge compaction in background.rs uses NOT IN (SELECT id  
  FROM entries) — matching only fully-deleted entries. Quarantined entries      
  (status=3) remain in entries, so their GRAPH_EDGES rows survive every tick.
  build_typed_relation_graph then emits a WARN for each such edge because it    
  builds an active-only snapshot. Both CoAccess and Supports edges are affected.
                                                                                
  Affected Files:
  - crates/unimatrix-server/src/background.rs — line 519–525 (production SQL),  
  line 2933–2943 (test helper SQL)                                              
                                                                                
  Proposed Fix:                                                                 
                                                                                
  DELETE FROM graph_edges                                                       
  WHERE source_id NOT IN (SELECT id FROM entries WHERE status != ?1)            
     OR target_id NOT IN (SELECT id FROM entries WHERE status != ?1)            
  with .bind(Status::Quarantined as u8 as i64) — matching the prune pass        
  convention in status.rs. Two-line change, one file. Also add a sync-warning   
  comment to the test helper SQL.                                               
                                                                                
  Design Review: APPROVED WITH NOTES (both notes are non-blocking)
  - Use bound parameter ?1 + .bind(Status::Quarantined as u8 as i64) rather than
   literal 3 — matches prune pass convention, guards against enum renumbering   
  - Add doc comment to run_graph_edges_compaction noting it must stay in sync   
  with the production DELETE                                                    
                                                                                
  Risk Assessment: Low. Strictly a superset of deletions — no valid edges are
  newly removed. Deprecated (status=1) entries are unaffected. Background tick  
  path, not hot path.                                             
                                                                                
  Missing Tests:                                                                
  - test_background_tick_compaction_removes_quarantined_source_edges — entry
  with status=3 as source, both CoAccess and Supports edges deleted             
  - test_background_tick_compaction_removes_quarantined_target_edges — entry
  with status=3 as target, same edge types                                      
                                                                                
  Knowledge stored: Lesson #3906 (compaction must filter status, not just entry
  existence), ADR #3907 (GRAPH_EDGES compaction status filter).

entries.title: GRAPH_EDGES compaction must exclude quarantined entries (status=3), not just fully-deleted entries
GRAPH_EDGES compaction DELETE must filter quarantined entries by status, not just existence
ADR-001 bugfix-458: GRAPH_EDGES compaction must filter on status != 3 (quarantined), not just entry existence
co_access promotion SELECT missing quarantine-status filter causes compaction/promotion oscillation
Multi-pass cleanup: all passes on the same reference table must apply identical status filters
